workflow.name: dev-harness
workflow.instruction: Run DevHarness as a deterministic inline workflow. Use branch steps, loop steps, parallel implementation steps, explicit join steps, and dynamic next targets only when the target ids are declared in this workflow.

input.prompt: Read the task context, gather local/project evidence, identify constraints, risks, open questions, and architecture-sensitive pressure. Return a research packet for attack. Use outcome ready_for_attack when the packet is ready, or blocked when implementation-critical input is missing.
Attack the research packet for missing evidence, unsafe assumptions, scope drift, sensitive surfaces, and unclear approval questions. Return approved, needs_revision, or blocked.

Show the cleaned research packet and attack verdict to the user. Continue only after explicit approval. Return approval approved to continue to architecture_draft. Return approval rejected when the research must be revised. Return approval blocked with a blocker when the workflow cannot continue safely.

Produce the minimal architecture decision or structural contract required by the approved research. If architecture work is not needed, record the explicit no-artifact decision. Use outcome ready_for_attack or blocked.

Attack the architecture decision or structural contract for ownership seams, dependency direction, contract surfaces, durable artifact pressure, and compatibility risk. Return approved, needs_revision, or blocked.

Show the architecture decision or structural contract to the user. Continue only after explicit approval when the slice is non-trivial or architecture-sensitive. Return approval approved to continue to planning_draft. Return approval rejected when architecture must be revised. Return approval blocked with a blocker when the workflow cannot continue safely.

Translate approved research and any architecture contract into an execution plan: file zones, implementer owners, reviewer plan, verification surfaces, rollback surfaces, docs/contract touchpoints, risks, and assumptions. Use outcome ready_for_attack or blocked.

Challenge the execution plan for entity coverage, one-owner-per-file-zone, project baseline, architecture artifact manifest, verification and rollback surfaces, sensitive surfaces, request-path/contract touchpoints, and max-detail leaks. Return approved, needs_revision, or blocked.

Show one cleaned execution plan to the user. Stop until explicit implementation approval. Return approval approved to run the approved implementation branches, approval rejected to revise planning, or approval blocked with a blocker when implementation cannot continue safely.

Implement the approved backend slice only. Preserve the frozen research, architecture, execution plan, and user constraints. Verify your slice enough for the implementation join. Return outcome implemented or blocked.

Implement the approved frontend slice only. Preserve the frozen research, architecture, execution plan, and user constraints. Verify your slice enough for the implementation join. Return outcome implemented or blocked.
Update only approved architecture artifacts or architecture handoff notes required by the execution plan. Do not redesign. Return outcome implemented or blocked.

Join all implementation branch outputs. Check that selected branches completed, verification evidence exists, and no branch widened scope. Return outcome ready_for_review when implementation evidence is complete, or blocked with a blocker when implementation evidence is incomplete.

Review implementation against the approved architecture decision, ownership seams, dependency direction, compatibility surfaces, and artifact obligations. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.

Review backend correctness, contracts, data flow, validation, failure handling, permissions, rollout safety, observability, and tests for the implemented slice. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.
Review frontend correctness, data flow, state handling, accessibility-relevant behavior, and tests for the implemented slice. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.
Review visual/product taste, copy fit, interaction polish, and user-facing coherence for frontend-relevant changes. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.
Review trust boundaries, injection surfaces, secret handling, permission assumptions, and unsafe automation paths for the implemented slice. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.
Review data minimization, sensitive content exposure, durable memory/logging risk, and user-context handling for the implemented slice. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.
Review verification depth, regression risk, edge cases, deterministic runtime behavior, and handoff quality for the implemented slice. Return outcome passed, needs_changes, or blocked.

Join all selected review branch outputs. If every required reviewer passes, return outcome passed. If implementation changes are needed, return outcome needs_changes. If review cannot finish safely, return outcome blocked with a blocker.
Workflow completed; return final summary and durable follow-up notes when applicable.
Workflow blocked; return the blocker, evidence, risk, and exact next decision or missing input.
